reasoning: The question asks if there will be >10 times the baseline rate of 'Explosions/Remote violence' in Guadeloupe. To resolve YES, there would need to be 11 or more artillery/IED/airstrike/remote explosives events in Guadeloupe within the 30 days leading up to March 8, 2026. Guadeloupe is not an active war zone, and web searches for 2025/2026 turn up absolutely no mention of armed conflict or remote violence. References to an "explosive situation" in the news pertain entirely to civil unrest and protests. Therefore, it is exceedingly unlikely that 11+ ACLED-defined 'Explosions/Remote violence' events will occur during this period.
